damage.turf: Wilted, brown, or loose turf that may peel back where roots are pruned
damage.secondary: Skunk, raccoon, bird, or armadillo feeding can indicate grub activity

scouting.method: Cut and peel back turf/soil samples and count larvae in the rootzone
scouting.timing: Late summer to early fall for many grub complexes; preventive timing is earlier

cultural_control.irrigation: Avoid drought stress where root feeding is active
cultural_control.recovery: Encourage rooting and recovery after control

chemical_control.notes: Preventive products must be timed before or near egg hatch. Curative products are better matched to small active larvae.

damage.turf: Small brown patches, clipped leaves, and thinning near the canopy
damage.signs: Silk-lined tunnels, green frass, or moth activity at dusk

scouting.method: Use a soap flush or inspect canopy/thatch for larvae and frass
scouting.timing: Warm-season activity periods when moth flights or feeding are observed

cultural_control.thatch: Manage excessive thatch where larvae shelter
cultural_control.mowing: Avoid unnecessary stress that slows recovery

chemical_control.notes: Surface-feeder products should be applied to the canopy target zone; watering-in needs differ by product.

damage.turf: Poa annua thinning, yellowing, and collapse, often at edges or stress zones
damage.signs: Larvae inside stems/crowns; adults can be monitored around overwintering movement

scouting.method: Inspect Poa annua crowns/stems and monitor adult movement where ABW is known
scouting.timing: Spring adult movement and larval development windows

cultural_control.host: Reduce reliance on Poa annua where practical
cultural_control.stress: Reduce stress on high-risk edges and collars

chemical_control.notes: ABW programs depend heavily on life stage and local monitoring.

damage.turf: Irregular yellow to brown drought-like patches
damage.pattern: Often worse in hot, dry, sunny areas

scouting.method: Use flotation or hands-and-knees inspection at patch margins
scouting.timing: Hot, dry periods when damage expands

cultural_control.water: Avoid drought stress where chinch bug pressure is active

chemical_control.notes: Target active insects and match product movement to the feeding zone.

damage.turf: Drought-like patches with stems that may break off or show sawdust-like frass
damage.signs: Larvae feed in stems/crowns and later roots

scouting.method: Inspect stems and crowns for larvae/frass; monitor adults where historically damaging
scouting.timing: Spring adult activity and larval feeding windows

cultural_control.cultivars: Use tolerant turf where billbugs are chronic
cultural_control.stress: Avoid drought stress during feeding periods

chemical_control.notes: Timing should target adults or young larvae before deep root feeding.

damage.turf: Small circular scars or pockmarks on greens and closely mowed turf
damage.signs: Burrows, clipped leaves, and larvae visible after flushing

scouting.method: Soap flush on greens/collars or inspect burrows and feeding scars
scouting.timing: Warm periods when surface feeding appears

cultural_control.clippings: Manage clippings and surface debris where larvae shelter
cultural_control.mowing: Monitor closely after mowing or when birds feed on greens

chemical_control.notes: Target the surface/canopy zone; avoid unnecessary watering-in unless the label calls for it.

damage.turf: Mounds can disrupt ball roll and scalp closely mowed turf
damage.signs: Visible mound activity on greens, collars, tees, or fairways

scouting.method: Map active mounds and prioritize playability/safety areas
scouting.timing: Warm periods with active mound building

cultural_control.surface: Disperse mounds before mowing where practical
cultural_control.threshold: Treat based on playability or safety thresholds rather than presence alone

chemical_control.notes: Stored KB lists bifenthrin for ants/surface insects.

damage.turf: Canopy thinning, clipped leaves, or localized surface-feeding injury
damage.signs: Larvae or insects present in canopy/thatch after flushing

scouting.method: Confirm the pest with soap flush or direct inspection before choosing a product
scouting.timing: Treat active feeding rather than calendar-only surface insect concerns

cultural_control.diagnosis: Confirm insect activity before treating; drought, disease, and traffic can mimic injury

chemical_control.notes: Use a product that matches the confirmed insect and feeding location.

damage.turf: Mounds disrupt playability and maintenance; stings are a safety issue

scouting.method: Map active mounds and prioritize playability and safety areas

cultural_control.threshold: Treat based on mound density and playability/safety needs
cultural_control.surface: Disperse mounds before mowing where practical

chemical_control.notes: Broadcast or mound programs depend on product and site needs.

damage.turf: Rapid canopy feeding and defoliation during outbreaks
damage.signs: Larvae and feeding injury often appear suddenly in hot weather

scouting.method: Soap flush and direct inspection of canopy for larvae

cultural_control.monitoring: Scout quickly when moth flights or sudden feeding appear
cultural_control.recovery: Support regrowth after defoliation with sound irrigation and fertility

chemical_control.notes: Surface-feeding caterpillar control should target active larvae.
